programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Michelle Ackerley and Iain Stirling present(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to create a garden incorporating a BMX track for youngster Ollie, who has yet to reach the age of 10. Oliver's family, from Appleton in Cheshire, has budgeted £3,000 for the budget, and it's going to need every penny of that to successfully transform the present bland suburban space into a cross between a race track for Oliver and a show garden for parents Sally and Ian(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Broadcaster Rylan Clark-Neal, comedian Sara Pascoe, retail consultant Mary Portas and comedian Paul Sinha join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The paramedics arrive at Dot's and as Bex is taken away in an ambulance, a stunned Louise watches on while Lisa is clearly racked with guilt. To make matters worse, Dotty mentions the pills found in Bex's room, leaving Louise shocked. Meanwhile, the Slaters remind Kush that Walford is no safe place for Martin, Sharon is desperate to meet Mel's demands and Jean is not prepared for what Daniel says to her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle in the last of the semi-finals, hoping to secure a place in finals week. The five remaining celebrities cook one dish of their own design for three of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Jay Rayner, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa - a daunting task for even the most adept chefs. Presenters John Torode and Gregg Wallace later determine which four will go through(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Karen and Geraldine are perplexed by who might have told the police about Mrs Bookham's missing money. However with Geraldine falsely accusing Lisa Marie, it's only a matter of time before they realise who the real culprit is. At the amusement arcade, the appearance of a last-minute candidate for a new staff vacancy causes some surprise. Meanwhile, Geraldine's financial worries look like they might be over when she meets with a mystery investor. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning Bridget Jones star Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael. Music is supplied by Elbow, who perform White Noise, White Heat(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The first episode of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, based on her one-woman stage show about a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living through a mixture of bravado and casual sex. Fleabag struggles to make ends meet in the frenetic world of contemporary London as she tries to work out what she should be doing with her life. With Olivia Colman and Hugh Dennis(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Leicester and Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England v Brazil (Kick-off 12.45pm). All the action from the friendly at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough. Phil Neville's side have defeated the Brazilians twice in the last 12 months, prevailing 2-1 in the SheBelieves Cup in February and winning a friendly 1-0 in Nottingham this time last year. However, the Lionesses are without a victory since their fourth-place finish in the World Cup, having since drawn 3-3 with Belgium and lost 2-1 against Norway, something they will be determined to put right back on home soil(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Shara Proctor begins her bid to go one better than her silver medal in 2015 when she takes part in the long jump qualifying, while the heats for the women's 100m hurdles and qualifying for the men's javelin also take place. With analysis from Jessica Ennis-Hill(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's European champion Laura Muir will hope to be in contention to win the world medal that narrowly eluded her two year ago in the 1500m, and finals also get under way in the men's shot put and women's triple jump. With analysis from Paula Radcliffe(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the third live episode of this year's series, as the fourteen remaining celebrities take to the dancefloor. This week, it's all about Hollywood, as the contestants and their partners hope to impress with a blockbuster performance. Judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the routines out of ten before the vote lines open - which gives viewers at home the chance to have their say(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Mason struggles to ask for help in his most difficult week as a junior doctor and Ethan cancels his assessment with Mason when Effie turns up wanting his advice about the clinical trial. David, Ollie and Rosa are all playing happy families, until David is shocked to learn his ex-wife Rosalene is demanding Ollie come back and live with her. Medical drama, starring Victor Oshin, Jason Durr and Ruth Madoc(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Seattle Seahawks v Los Angeles Rams. Plus, a look at the major stories shaping the NFL world(n)

programme.title: Old School (2003)
programme.desc: An embittered lawyer decides to relive the wild, crazy days of his youth after his girlfriend is unfaithful, and enlists the help of two equally disillusioned friends in setting up a fraternity house next to his former college campus. Comedy, starring Luke Wilson, Will Ferrell, Vince Vaughn and Juliette Lewis(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Single mum Lisa hopes Australia holds the key to a brighter future for her and her daughter, Jessica, so they spend a week sampling life there before deciding whether to move(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from Newark Antiques and Collectors Fair in Nottinghamshire, inviting two teams of friends to purchase items and make the greater profit when they go under the hammer at Charles Hanson's saleroom in Derby. Experts Natasha Raskin and Ben Cooper assist the contenders(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits St George's cathedral in Hertfordshire to meet members of the UK's Coptic Orthodox community, the roots of which can be traced back to first-century Egypt(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Martel Maxwell meet the new owners of a bungalow in Callington in Cornwall and a terrace in Watford, and hear their plans for their auction buys(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple search for a property in Shropshire with a budget of £450,000. While in the area, Ginny takes a tour of Weston Park House(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a rural back garden with a steep slope in Somerset, each hoping their plan will make the most of the countryside views(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A team at the Bristol Upholstery Collective get to work on an old three-piece suite, while woodworking duo Josh and Oli try to make something of an old oak beam(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1980s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are Carol Decker, Dennis Seaton, Bob Champion, Jo Durie, Samantha Fox, Duncan Norvelle, John Kettley and Lizzie Webb(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, singer and actor Harry Connick Jr performs Cole Porter's Broadway classic Anything Goes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: In devastated Warsaw, Kasia makes plans to flee with her grief-stricken mother Maria, but gets caught in a resistance cell's attack on German soldiers. Harry returns to Manchester, but when he finally plucks up the courage to tell Lois the truth she disarms him by declaring her love, while Tom shocks Douglas by announcing he is now a conscientious objector. In Berlin, Nancy is frustrated with the growing power of the censor, while Webster and Albert's joy is overshadowed by the growing threat of prejudice they face(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Etihad Stadium, Arsenal v Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium, Southampton v Chelsea at St Mary's Stadium and Newcastle United v Manchester United at St James' Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: NFL Highlights
programme.desc: Chicago Bears v Oakland Raiders. Action from the inter-conference clash, as Tottenham Hotspur Stadium hosted an NFL match for the first time(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the penultimate leg of their journey, heading towards an auction in Dunfermline, Scotland(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Police from the UK and Uganda trace a woman who fled Britain after killing her daughter, and officers in London are on the hunt for a man wanted in Italy for committing rape and serious assault. Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost gives advice on how to prune overgrown evergreen shrubs and adds to his borders to create height. Rachel de Thame shows how to maintain containers and refresh with plants that will extend the display into autumn, and there's a feature on a gardener whose frequent absences from home have influenced the design and planting of his garden(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. On the road, Nadiya joins the team Tate and Lyle on the River Thames, to find out how they produce one of her favourite fast-tracks to flavour - golden syrup(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Adam Fleming and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives, ideal to draw upon when planning the perfect picnic. The menu includes Paul Hollywood's pork pies with quail eggs, a superb sandwich from James Martin and Ainsley's own mustard, bacon and onion tarts(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House proud couples from Northamptonshire and Surrey swap homes and give their verdicts on the lifestyles of complete strangers. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: The Sky's the Limit (1943)
programme.desc: A Second World War hero returns home to New York and sneaks into town incognito to avoid a big fuss. He meets the girl of his dreams, but his efforts to court her are thwarted when she leaps to the conclusion that he is a coward trying to get out of going to war. Romantic musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire and Joan Leslie, with an appearance by bandleader Freddie Slack(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson uncover a few gems including a silver ladle with a surprise in its bowl and a train set brought in by the son of William Moorcroft, the world-famous ceramicist. Plus, Paul finds out about the epic 18th-century voyage by the second Englishman to circumnavigate the globe(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day eight of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Home favourite Mutaz Essa Barshim is his nation's only defending world champion, and will be looking to take high jump gold once again this evening, despite recent injury issues. Likewise, South Africa's Wayde van Niekerk will be hoping to put his own injury troubles behind him to prevail once again in the 400m. Medals are also decided in the women's 400m hurdles, in which 20-year-old American Sydney McLaughlin was the pre-event favourite, and finals take place in the women's discus and men's 3,000m steeplechase. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Mary Berry comes to tea with Monty Don and, as well has giving her a tour of Longmeadow, he offers his advice on her new garden. Frances Tophill meets gardeners on an allotment in Birmingham and harvests some unusual vegetables, while Adam Frost and Arit Anderson join forces to transform a front garden in Hampshire into a haven for the owners as well as wildlife. In Nottinghamshire, the programme meets an enthusiast who has amassed a collection of over 100 varieties of rhubarb and in Scotland a herb grower with over 600 different types(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into the topic of Questions and Qualifications, with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Ade Adepitan, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, with Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: A programme from Glasgow, focusing on the cultural influence of food, exploring what people eat can say about who they are as individuals, communities and nations? Meanwhile, presenter Mary Beard is join by Great British Bake Off judge Prue Leith to take a tour of an exhibition of art and artefacts at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, which explores food culture in ancient Pompeii(n)

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: The lives of composer Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, whose romance began on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of foreign artists, writers, and musicians. This film follows their relationship, from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, to how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician. Along the way the film tells of the tragedy that befell those that could not survive the beauty of Hydra, the highs and lows of Leonard's career, and the inspirational power that Marianne possessed(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: The chefs face their final challenge in Paris, where they must prove their worth in a luxury restaurant run by the son of one of Jason Atherton's culinary heroes. As the brigade prepares to deliver the finest in French haute cuisine, expert advisors Dale Bainbridge and Andrew Beynon offer them an added incentive - the best performing member of the group will go home with a volume of all their best recipes(n)

programme.title: Seahorse: The Dad Who Gave Birth (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about a gay transgender man's efforts to have a baby, following his story over the course of three years from the initial struggle to conceive to the eventual birth, and examining the challenges and prejudices he has faced, forcing him to rely on the support of friends and family in the face of mounting hostility(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: Ali-A visits Portsmouth to help fashion-mad Molly revamp her dad's 20-year-old family car, with help from motoring guru Sideways Sid, and professional designer Alicja Teper(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Bee Movie (2007)
programme.desc: A bee wearies of a life dedicated to producing honey and decides to leave his hive. During his foray into the wider world, he befriends a florist. However, when he realises what becomes of the honey his species toils so hard to make, he resolves to sue the entire human race for stealing from his fellow creatures. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jerry Seinfeld, Renee Zellweger and Chris Rock(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon host the revival of the programme in which radio-controlled robots created by teams of amateur engineers battle it out in an arena. The machines have to make it through an initial group stage, then head-to-head rounds before the heat final. The first show features students from Reading and a family team from Caithness, as well as a memorable robot from the original series(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the capabilities of bears, who can live in practically every habitat on Earth due to being able to endure extreme conditions. The naturalist examines the specialised adaptations that have enabled the mammals to thrive, including how the hollow fur of polar bears allows them to feed during gruelling winters(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Competing in Cardiff for a place in the final are a renovated, three-storey semi-detached, a period semi with a modern twist and a rustic, shabby-chic home with a love of upcycling(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Colin Jackson invites chef Brian Turner to accompany him to Cardiff's fresh food market, where his mother used to take him to as a child, and he still shops at today. Jackson's culinary upbringing combined the staples of Caribbean cuisine with produce from the local larder and Brian finds plenty to inspire him as he sets about creating a dish full of nostalgia for the former professional athlete(n)

programme.title: Fast Girls (2012)
programme.desc: A fierce rivalry develops between a talented working class sprinter and the wealthy daughter of a former Olympic medal-winner as they compete on the track. They are forced to settle their differences and work together when they are recruited for the same relay team. Drama, with Lenora Crichlow, Lily James, Rupert Graves and Noel Clarke, who also co-wrote the script(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Linford Christie joins comedy star Brian Conley for a nostalgic look at the archive television that helped set him on the path to success as an Olympic sprinter, from the crazy kicks and amazing flicks of 1970s wrestling to the antics of Bob and Terry in The Likely Lads, via the world's first superstar footballer on and off the small screen(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin visits Sandon Hall with Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson, and hears about some of the pioneering women connected to the venue(n)

programme.title: The Best of British Takeaways
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge travels the country to find out what makes the best and the trade secrets behind Chinese takeaways, while Cherry Healey explores the stories that shaped the UK's communities as well as the population's palates. Three of the nation's award-winning Chinese takeaways are also invited to take part in a contest in the heart of London's China Town. Each believes their version of the takeaway is the best, but it's down to Tom and his expert judge, writer and chef Ching He-Huang, to decide a winner(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: As the nation's trading relationships face their biggest change in decades, Ed Balls, Ade Adepitan and Cherry Healey explore the science and systems that enable Britain to import and export goods around the world on an unprecedented scale. Ed is at the London Gateway port to reveal all the tricks of the fruit and vegetable trade, Ade visits Peru to see how Britain's obsession with healthy eating has created a booming economy for avocado growers, and Cherry meets a British farmer keeping the Middle East supplied with apples(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A night at the cinema with the rest of the platoon and a black-market toasted cheese sandwich make Mainwaring have an unusual dream in which he is Napoleon at the Battle of Waterloo. Comedy first broadcast in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents the concluding coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. The British teams will hope to be in the mix for medals once again this evening in the finals of the women's and men's 4x100m relays, having won silver and gold respectively two years ago in London. The final of the women's 5,000m is also contested, a discipline that has been won by an athlete from either Kenya or Ethiopia in every staging since 2005. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Singer Adam Ant presents a retrospective look at the pop culture of 1981, when Kim Wilde, Clare Grogan and Tom Selleck were among the pin-ups. It was the year Britain was introduced to animated crimefighter Danger Mouse, the sport of darts was in its heyday, and the films Gregory's Girl and Porky's hit cinemas(n)

programme.title: La La Land (2016)
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend(n)

programme.title: This Beautiful Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman who dreams of being a children's author forges an unlikely friendship with a cantankerous, rich old widower, as well as his housekeeper and an inventor who frequents the library where she works. Fantasy drama, starring Jessica Brown Findlay, Tom Wilkinson, Andrew Scott and Jeremy Irvine(n)

programme.title: Papadopoulos & Sons (2012)
programme.desc: A Greek millionaire loses everything he has in a financial crisis, leaving him with only one asset - his share in the fish and chip shop his father used to run. He is forced to return to his roots and work with his laidback brother to revive the family business. Comedy, starring Stephen Dillane, Georgia Groome and Georges Corraface(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don celebrates the berries of the season and plants up potfuls of colour for spring. Frances Tophill meets gardeners on an allotment in Birmingham and harvests some unusual vegetables, while Adam Frost and Arit Anderson join forces to transform a front garden in Hampshire into a haven for the owners as well as wildlife. In Nottinghamshire, the programme meets an enthusiast who has amassed a collection of over 100 varieties of rhubarb and in Scotland a herb grower with over 600 different types(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is in Cumbria to explore the Lake District's wild side. meeting a man who runs pony treks that follow old pack-horse routes across the fells. Joe then hears more about the region's industrial heritage as he meets artists behind a new installation celebrating the area's copper mining history. Plus, Charlotte Smith investigating the rise of far-right extremism in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the 17th staging of the event, as thousands of runners line up in front of Cardiff Castle to tackle the flat course. A strong African showing is expected, with Wilson Chebet, John Lotiang and Shadrack Kimining set to compete in the men's race, and Paskalia Kipkoech, Lucy Cheruiyot and Lydia Mathathi among the entrants in the women's competition. Commentary comes from John Rawling and Tanni Grey-Thompson, with reports by Lowri Morgan and Jamie Baulch(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar becomes the prime suspect for a series of robberies carried out in his own distinctive style, and sets out to clear his name by catching the copycat crook responsible. His quest takes him to the Cote d'Azur, where he encounters the demure daughter of a wealthy holiday-maker, and before long the two are drawn together. Alfred Hitchcock's Oscar-winning romantic mystery, starring Cary Grant, Grace Kelly and Jessie Royce Landis(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: A revealing and fascinating insight into this troubled legend of cinema, who appeared as comfortable in romantic comedies as he was in iconic Hitchcock thrillers. Featuring newly discovered footage shot by Grant himself and readings from his unpublished autobiography, spoken by actor Jonathan Pryce(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils. Action from Semi-Final 2 at DW Stadium, as the sides battled it out for a place in the Grand Final(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 10th and final day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Norwegian teenage sensation Jakob Ingebrigtsen is expected to go toe-to-toe with Kenya's Timothy Cheruiyot in the 1500m final, with Great Britain's Charlie Da'Vall Grice also hoping to be involved. The men's 10,000m also takes place, and the women's long jump and men's javelin finals get under way in the field. With analysis from Paula Radcliffe(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of the 10th and final day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. A new champion will be crowned in the 100m hurdles, as 2017 winner Sally Pearson has since retired. The championships then conclude with the women's and men's 4x400m finals, in which Great Britain will be hoping for a strong showing after earning respective silver and bronze medals two years ago(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of more business ideas, including an eco-friendly range of body and skincare products, a divisive savoury snack, and a brand of fruit-flavoured water. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Unsure until they share their responses, Tom and Louise discuss the rekindling of their relationship. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sheepdog trainer Katy invites three potential partners to experience a taste of country life in the Cumbrian Fells. While the weather is bleak, painter and decorator John, motorbike champion David and former footballer Carl take to herding sheep and mucking out kennels with remarkable success. Grace takes a day off from her family's dairy to visit a hobby farmer on his own turf, while Sara Cox pays a visit to Cumbrian farmer David to deliver letters from six candidates for a speed dating session(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Wallasey, Wirral in Merseyside, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo peeks behind the scenes of Radio 1's Live Lounge, the twice-weekly feature attracting an array of some of the biggest and best pop artists on the planet, selecting tracks from sessions by Taylor Swift, Yungblud and Mahalia(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition first aired on July 28, with performers including Pat Benatar, Shakin' Stevens and Glenn Medeiros(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri(n)

programme.title: Betty Davis: Godmother of Funk
programme.desc: Profile of the aspiring songwriter, who arrived on the '70s scene with her daring personality, iconic fashion and outrageous funk music. She befriended Jimi Hendrix and Sly Stone, wrote songs for the Chambers Brothers and the Commodores, and married Miles Davis - turning him from jazz to funk on the album she named Bitches Brew. Betty was a feminist pioneer, inspiring and intimidating in a manner like no woman before. This documentary charts her rise from humble upbringings to take the genre by storm, revealing the mystery of her subsequent disappearance and her battle with mental illness(n)

programme.title: The Story of Funk: One Nation Under a Groove
programme.desc: Documentary examining the history of funk, starting from its birth in the black community at a time of self-discovery, struggle and social change. The fun style of the music changed the streets of the US in the seventies with new trends in fashion and slang emerging, and its celebration of African-American culture. The genre produced some of the most famous acts in the world, such as James Brown, Sly & the Family Stone, Earth, Wind & Fire, and Kool & the Gang. Including interviews with George Clinton, Ray Parker Jr and trombonist Fred Wesley(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition first aired on July 28, with performers including Pat Benatar, Shakin' Stevens and Glenn Medeiros(n)

programme.title: Hot Chocolate at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC performances and rare interview extracts celebrating British pop group Hot Chocolate and in particular its lead soloist Errol Brown. The band's top 10 hits from the 1970s and early 1980s are showcased, alongside rarely seen early performances and cult fan favourites(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cameraman Kim Wolhuter offers a remarkable new insight into the life of the cheetah, spending almost two years walking alongside a mother and her young family to unravel in intimate detail what it takes to turn tiny cubs into accomplished predators. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The country's diverse wildlife, beginning by exploring how rural Chinese peasants in the south have developed a relationship with the region's animal inhabitants - including how water buffalo are used in agriculture, and tamed cormorants help their owners to fish(n)

programme.title: The King's Choice (2016)
programme.desc: With the German army advancing on Norway, King Haakon VII faces a decision that will change his country forever. Historical drama, starring Jesper Christensen, Anders Baasmo Christiansen and Karl Markovics. In Norwegian, Danish and German(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Andy has a job interview and Lance has a lot of catching up to do, so the mystery of the buried bomber is the least of their problems. But then a disturbing possibility emerges from an unlikely source, throwing everything into doubt. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook and Toby Jones(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: After recent revelations, Lance, Andy, Becky and Sophie are trying to come to terms with an uncertain future. Terry puts his emergency plan into action - but it will help only one of them. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook and Toby Jones(n)

programme.desc: Terry is expecting a big turnout for the DMDC's annual rally. But it remains to be seen whether the day will bring heartache or triumph for Andy and Lance(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cameraman Kim Wolhuter offers a remarkable new insight into the life of the cheetah, spending almost two years walking alongside a mother and her young family to unravel in intimate detail what it takes to turn tiny cubs into accomplished predators. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson, reveal that eugenics - the controversial idea that was a driving force behind the Nazi death camps - originated in the upper echelons of the British scientific community. They also examine its legacy and continued influence today(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a pioneering team in Brazil as they track individual wild jaguars in order to gain new insights into their lives. However, when two young cubs are orphaned, the team must use its new-found knowledge to help them return to the wild(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Film-maker Chris Terrill captures an intimate and personal portrait of life on board ultra-modern super aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth, as its crew learns the intricacies of a warship that will change the way Britain goes to war. In this first instalment, early 2016 sees sailors begin to arrive in Rosyth dockyard in Scotland where the ship is still in construction, but nearing completion. Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors are assembling to take the prototype warship to sea for the first time. These trials herald what should be 50 years of service(n)

programme.title: Jumbo: The Plane That Changed the World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins and development of the Boeing 747, which was the world's first wide-bodied commercial airliner and revolutionised air travel after coming into service in 1970. In interviews filmed on the original prototype, chief engineer Joe Sutter and his team describe the challenges they faced, from exploding engines to vibrations that threatened to tear the aircraft apart, and test pilot Brien Wygle reveals how on the first flight they hooked up car batteries as back-ups in case of engine failure(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory(n)

programme.title: Operation Jericho
programme.desc: Martin Shaw tells the story of Operation Jericho, a 1944 mission that saw a squadron of RAF Mosquito bombers try to free French Resistance fighters from a prison in Amiens by destroying its walls. He takes to the air to retrace the British pilots' journey and meets survivors and aircrew as he tries to piece together who originally ordered the raid(n)

programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children(n)

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: In an attempt to apologise to Kari for eating the cake she made him as a surprise, Barry tries to be kind in lots of different ways by becoming Captain Kind Kid(n)

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig visits the dentist to have her teeth counted, but Woolly has to encourage her when she refuses to open her mouth(n)

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: The children learn about their families, their heritage and their family trees. Bella is worried her family might be boring compared with Sam and Mali's clans(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly decides to make a film with Alice to cheer her up, but all sorts of things go wrong and then Mack manages to erase the film completely. How can Molly save the day?(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Grannie Island battles to win the Grand Struay Sheep Contest for the eighth year in a row with her beloved sheep Alecina(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Flamingo and John give Sarah and Duck a tour of their home and invite them to play their favourite game(n)

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The children have to do a few chores before Charlie can watch his favourite TV show(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo keeps getting calls on her mobile phone, and after a while Tee gets fed up because he cannot attract her attention(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A Skymoo accidentally knocks the Iron Chicken out of her nest, and falling to the surface of the Clangers' planet she hurts her wing. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines look for the perfect place to have a picnic, and invite the Wottingers to join in the fun(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain reads My Little Star by Mark Sperring(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a property in Birmingham with some mesmerising wallpaper and a south Wales house that has her dancing with joy. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a place that has him a bit confused(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for a cocaine smuggler in the north-west of England leads to an arrest in a wealthy suburb of Accra in Ghana(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: A case in which a single mother was left terrified after her car broke down leaving her and her children stranded. The court has since said she should get her money back, but tempers flare when the enforcement officers visit the garage responsible. Plus, Billy and Michael have an early morning encounter with a particularly abusive debtor(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury fair, Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. Natasha also learns more about one of the UK's most-loved cartoonists(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara's acrimony takes an unexpected turn as Valerie reaches out to Jimmi. Ruhma comes across an expectant mother who is doing everything not to give birth despite being in labour(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property hunting in North Devon for a couple whose search for a country home on a £325,000 budget has so far fallen short. He also learns about a unique local pigment, known as Bideford Black, and enjoys the artistic freedom of painting on the beach(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Michelle Ackerley and Iain Stirling present(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams observes gulls rearing chicks and causing havoc on the streets of Aberystwyth, and on the outskirts of town, badgers are dodging cars on a busy main road as they set off on a nightly expedition for food in the town's housing estates. In Conwy, jackdaws have set up home on the castle walls. Pembroke Castle too has its jackdaws, and a beautiful mill pond that attracts otter families looking for eels(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Broadcaster Rylan Clark-Neal, comedian Sara Pascoe, retail consultant Mary Portas and comedian Paul Sinha join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The paramedics arrive at Dot's and as Bex is taken away in an ambulance, a stunned Louise watches on while Lisa is clearly racked with guilt. To make matters worse, Dotty mentions the pills found in Bex's room, leaving Louise shocked. Meanwhile, the Slaters remind Kush that Walford is no safe place for Martin, Sharon is desperate to meet Mel's demands and Jean is not prepared for what Daniel says to her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle in the last of the semi-finals, hoping to secure a place in finals week. The five remaining celebrities cook one dish of their own design for three of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Jay Rayner, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa - a daunting task for even the most adept chefs. Presenters John Torode and Gregg Wallace later determine which four will go through(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Karen and Geraldine are perplexed by who might have told the police about Mrs Bookham's missing money. However with Geraldine falsely accusing Lisa Marie, it's only a matter of time before they realise who the real culprit is. At the amusement arcade, the appearance of a last-minute candidate for a new staff vacancy causes some surprise. Meanwhile, Geraldine's financial worries look like they might be over when she meets with a mystery investor. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning Bridget Jones star Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael. Music is supplied by Elbow, who perform White Noise, White Heat(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me
programme.desc: Documentary following former Wales rugby star Gareth Thomas as he opens up about living with HIV and sets about tackling the stigmas, myths and misunderstandings surrounding the virus. The programme follows Gareth as he prepares to take on the biggest physical challenge of his career - running the world's toughest Iron Man(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The first episode of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, based on her one-woman stage show about a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living through a mixture of bravado and casual sex. Fleabag struggles to make ends meet in the frenetic world of contemporary London as she tries to work out what she should be doing with her life. With Olivia Colman and Hugh Dennis(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Carl Clarke, and singer Mika, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: The comedian and Mandy Starr head to Neath Working Men's Social Club to discover the pulling power of tribute acts inspired by stars from Tina Turner to Tom Jones. They meet two sisters who have been coming to the club weekly for half a century, and Owen has a lesson in bingo etiquette(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Leicester and Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England v Brazil (Kick-off 12.45pm). All the action from the friendly at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough. Phil Neville's side have defeated the Brazilians twice in the last 12 months, prevailing 2-1 in the SheBelieves Cup in February and winning a friendly 1-0 in Nottingham this time last year. However, the Lionesses are without a victory since their fourth-place finish in the World Cup, having since drawn 3-3 with Belgium and lost 2-1 against Norway, something they will be determined to put right back on home soil(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Shara Proctor begins her bid to go one better than her silver medal in 2015 when she takes part in the long jump qualifying, while the heats for the women's 100m hurdles and qualifying for the men's javelin also take place. With analysis from Jessica Ennis-Hill(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's European champion Laura Muir will hope to be in contention to win the world medal that narrowly eluded her two year ago in the 1500m, and finals also get under way in the men's shot put and women's triple jump. With analysis from Paula Radcliffe(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the third live episode of this year's series, as the fourteen remaining celebrities take to the dancefloor. This week, it's all about Hollywood, as the contestants and their partners hope to impress with a blockbuster performance. Judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the routines out of ten before the vote lines open - which gives viewers at home the chance to have their say(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Mason struggles to ask for help in his most difficult week as a junior doctor and Ethan cancels his assessment with Mason when Effie turns up wanting his advice about the clinical trial. David, Ollie and Rosa are all playing happy families, until David is shocked to learn his ex-wife Rosalene is demanding Ollie come back and live with her. Medical drama, starring Victor Oshin, Jason Durr and Ruth Madoc(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Seattle Seahawks v Los Angeles Rams. Plus, a look at the major stories shaping the NFL world(n)

programme.title: Old School (2003)
programme.desc: An embittered lawyer decides to relive the wild, crazy days of his youth after his girlfriend is unfaithful, and enlists the help of two equally disillusioned friends in setting up a fraternity house next to his former college campus. Comedy, starring Luke Wilson, Will Ferrell, Vince Vaughn and Juliette Lewis(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lisa is keen to move abroad for a new life in Australia and away from the stresses of running a village pub to allow for a change of pace. However, the relocation would require her husband James to come to terms with being separated from his daughter, and the family must make their decision after sampling life in the city of Melbourne(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the 17th staging of the event, as thousands of runners line up in front of Cardiff Castle to tackle the flat course. A strong African showing is expected, with Wilson Chebet, John Lotiang and Shadrack Kimining set to compete in the men's race, and Paskalia Kipkoech, Lucy Cheruiyot and Lydia Mathathi among the entrants in the women's competition. Commentary comes from John Rawling and Tanni Grey-Thompson, with reports by Lowri Morgan and Jamie Baulch(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Thomas Plant join two teams as they try to find valuable items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, with two boxing instructors competing against a Texan couple. Meanwhile, Tim Wonnacott visits Ormesby Hall near Middlesbrough(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits St George's cathedral in Hertfordshire to meet members of the UK's Coptic Orthodox community, the roots of which can be traced back to first-century Egypt(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Martel Maxwell meet the new owners of a bungalow in Callington in Cornwall and a terrace in Watford, and hear their plans for their auction buys(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking to move to the rural setting of the Scottish Borders on a budget of £850,000, having lived in the city of Brussels for the last 20 years(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a rural back garden with a steep slope in Somerset, each hoping their plan will make the most of the countryside views(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A team at the Bristol Upholstery Collective get to work on an old three-piece suite, while woodworking duo Josh and Oli try to make something of an old oak beam(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams observes gulls rearing chicks and causing havoc on the streets of Aberystwyth, and on the outskirts of town, badgers are dodging cars on a busy main road as they set off on a nightly expedition for food in the town's housing estates. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1980s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are Carol Decker, Dennis Seaton, Bob Champion, Jo Durie, Samantha Fox, Duncan Norvelle, John Kettley and Lizzie Webb(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, singer and actor Harry Connick Jr performs Cole Porter's Broadway classic Anything Goes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: In devastated Warsaw, Kasia makes plans to flee with her grief-stricken mother Maria, but gets caught in a resistance cell's attack on German soldiers. Harry returns to Manchester, but when he finally plucks up the courage to tell Lois the truth she disarms him by declaring her love, while Tom shocks Douglas by announcing he is now a conscientious objector. In Berlin, Nancy is frustrated with the growing power of the censor, while Webster and Albert's joy is overshadowed by the growing threat of prejudice they face(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Etihad Stadium, Arsenal v Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium, Southampton v Chelsea at St Mary's Stadium and Newcastle United v Manchester United at St James' Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)
