programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, singer and actor Harry Connick Jr performs Cole Porter's Broadway classic Anything Goes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: In devastated Warsaw, Kasia makes plans to flee with her grief-stricken mother Maria, but gets caught in a resistance cell's attack on German soldiers. Harry returns to Manchester, but when he finally plucks up the courage to tell Lois the truth she disarms him by declaring her love, while Tom shocks Douglas by announcing he is now a conscientious objector. In Berlin, Nancy is frustrated with the growing power of the censor, while Webster and Albert's joy is overshadowed by the growing threat of prejudice they face(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Etihad Stadium, Arsenal v Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium, Southampton v Chelsea at St Mary's Stadium and Newcastle United v Manchester United at St James' Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland is joined by Michael Stewart and Steven Thompson to present action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Livingston v Celtic and Rangers v Hamilton Academical(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a property in Birmingham with some mesmerising wallpaper and a south Wales house that has her dancing with joy. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a place that has him a bit confused(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for a cocaine smuggler in the north-west of England leads to an arrest in a wealthy suburb of Accra in Ghana(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: A case in which a single mother was left terrified after her car broke down leaving her and her children stranded. The court has since said she should get her money back, but tempers flare when the enforcement officers visit the garage responsible. Plus, Billy and Michael have an early morning encounter with a particularly abusive debtor(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury fair, Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. Natasha also learns more about one of the UK's most-loved cartoonists(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara's acrimony takes an unexpected turn as Valerie reaches out to Jimmi. Ruhma comes across an expectant mother who is doing everything not to give birth despite being in labour(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property hunting in North Devon for a couple whose search for a country home on a £325,000 budget has so far fallen short. He also learns about a unique local pigment, known as Bideford Black, and enjoys the artistic freedom of painting on the beach(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Michelle Ackerley and Iain Stirling present(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to create a garden incorporating a BMX track for youngster Ollie, who has yet to reach the age of 10. Oliver's family, from Appleton in Cheshire, has budgeted £3,000 for the budget, and it's going to need every penny of that to successfully transform the present bland suburban space into a cross between a race track for Oliver and a show garden for parents Sally and Ian(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Broadcaster Rylan Clark-Neal, comedian Sara Pascoe, retail consultant Mary Portas and comedian Paul Sinha join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The paramedics arrive at Dot's and as Bex is taken away in an ambulance, a stunned Louise watches on while Lisa is clearly racked with guilt. To make matters worse, Dotty mentions the pills found in Bex's room, leaving Louise shocked. Meanwhile, the Slaters remind Kush that Walford is no safe place for Martin, Sharon is desperate to meet Mel's demands and Jean is not prepared for what Daniel says to her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle in the last of the semi-finals, hoping to secure a place in finals week. The five remaining celebrities cook one dish of their own design for three of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Jay Rayner, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa - a daunting task for even the most adept chefs. Presenters John Torode and Gregg Wallace later determine which four will go through(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Karen and Geraldine are perplexed by who might have told the police about Mrs Bookham's missing money. However with Geraldine falsely accusing Lisa Marie, it's only a matter of time before they realise who the real culprit is. At the amusement arcade, the appearance of a last-minute candidate for a new staff vacancy causes some surprise. Meanwhile, Geraldine's financial worries look like they might be over when she meets with a mystery investor. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning Bridget Jones star Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael. Music is supplied by Elbow, who perform White Noise, White Heat(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The first episode of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, based on her one-woman stage show about a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living through a mixture of bravado and casual sex. Fleabag struggles to make ends meet in the frenetic world of contemporary London as she tries to work out what she should be doing with her life. With Olivia Colman and Hugh Dennis(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Carl Clarke, and singer Mika, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads behind the scenes at Heathrow to see how the restaurants and cafes feed the 80 million people that pass through the airport's terminals each year. Back in her kitchen, she whips up dishes including a rich chicken korma, an apple sandwich cake, and some roast scallops(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Leicester and Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England v Brazil (Kick-off 12.45pm). All the action from the friendly at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough. Phil Neville's side have defeated the Brazilians twice in the last 12 months, prevailing 2-1 in the SheBelieves Cup in February and winning a friendly 1-0 in Nottingham this time last year. However, the Lionesses are without a victory since their fourth-place finish in the World Cup, having since drawn 3-3 with Belgium and lost 2-1 against Norway, something they will be determined to put right back on home soil(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Shara Proctor begins her bid to go one better than her silver medal in 2015 when she takes part in the long jump qualifying, while the heats for the women's 100m hurdles and qualifying for the men's javelin also take place. With analysis from Jessica Ennis-Hill(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of the ninth and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's European champion Laura Muir will hope to be in contention to win the world medal that narrowly eluded her two year ago in the 1500m, and finals also get under way in the men's shot put and women's triple jump. With analysis from Paula Radcliffe(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the third live episode of this year's series, as the fourteen remaining celebrities take to the dancefloor. This week, it's all about Hollywood, as the contestants and their partners hope to impress with a blockbuster performance. Judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the routines out of ten before the vote lines open - which gives viewers at home the chance to have their say(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Mason struggles to ask for help in his most difficult week as a junior doctor and Ethan cancels his assessment with Mason when Effie turns up wanting his advice about the clinical trial. David, Ollie and Rosa are all playing happy families, until David is shocked to learn his ex-wife Rosalene is demanding Ollie come back and live with her. Medical drama, starring Victor Oshin, Jason Durr and Ruth Madoc(n)

programme.desc: The story of a 10-year-old boy in Nazi-occupied Norway who supported his family after his father's death by working as a fisherman, and took a stand against the invaders(n)

programme.desc: Seamus O'Sullivan performs Hornpipes at The Trad Awards 2017(n)

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive(n)

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy and Dòmhnall Eòghainn MacKinnon introduces sets from Celtic Connections, 2018. This show features musicians Declan O'Rourke, Laura Cortese and Lahira(n)

programme.desc: A woman runs a hotel on the west coast and feels trapped in a loveless marriage - until the arrival of a young surfer. Drama, starring Kate Nic Chonaonaigh(n)

programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming, and swims in the River Truim, which flows north in Badenoch into the Spey(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Dance duo Twist and Pulse guest ahead of the Britain's Got Talent: The Champions final. Plus, a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Leigh, Greater Manchester, sort the treasures from their trash. A Chinese sauce boat catches the eye of dealer Jo Brayshaw, while Mark Stevens smells the prospect of a profit in the shape of some perfume bottles. Meanwhile, James Layte's attention is diverted towards a historic Almanac(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: The Gandhi family answer top 10 questions on subjects including EastEnders, The Jungle Book and Formula One, as they try to score the 'Perfect 10'. Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Karen, Ben, Ali and David work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla and Andrea lock horns, Tracy hears the worst news, and Graham attempts to make amends(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dev is stunned to find Asha has been trying to lighten her skin, and Michelle tells Robert how Ray made a pass at her and is now making her life hell. Meanwhile, Kevin tells Debbie that he can't accept the money their Aunt Vi left(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: After finding an unusual tattoo on the college dean's body and two more murder victims with the same inking, Lewis and Hathaway discover that the tattoo signifies membership of a secret esoteric society that believes past sins can be forgiven by the literal sharing of guilt. Before the fourth and final murder takes place, the team must uncover the guilty secret that the victims share in order to learn the identity of the killer. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott head the cast of this action drama, now well into its third and final season. Murtaugh (Wayans) takes a family holiday, but is pulled back into work after witnessing an alleged abduction. Meanwhile, Cole (Scott) is left to investigate a murder by himself. Later, however, the two cases begin to intersect. Then, Erica's mother, Senator Malick comes to town and stirs things up between her daughter, Cole and the case at hand. Kevin Rahm, Johnathan Fernandez and Michelle Mitchenor also star(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Builder Rich from Birmingham, blacksmith Andy from Glastonbury, business manager Ashley from London and estate agent Ross from Huddersfield all vie for the attentions of 30 single women, intent on capturing their interest and securing a date. However, first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness monitors their progress and reviews the dating action from last week's couples on the Isle of Fernando's(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: South Africa v Italy. Jill Douglas presents action from the Pool B encounter at Shizuoka Stadium Ecopa in Japan. With analysis from David Flatman and Shane Williams, and commentary by Miles Harrison(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: As police comb the Le Saux haulage yard for clues, Nancy realises to her horror that she's left incriminating evidence at the scene. Worse follows when she learns her mother has gone missing from her psychiatric ward. In due course, as the shocking contents of the truck are revealed, the case seems close to being solved. However, Nancy knows there's more - and that the killer is still at large. Karla Crome, Robert James-Collier and Lindsey Coulson star(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: There's an alert at HMP Downview women's prison when repeat offender Collette O'Flaherty, distraught at missing out on a place at the prisoners' family day, takes a fellow inmate hostage. A prisoner at HMP Portland hears false rumours that he is a sex offender - and suspects the culprit spreading the lies is a new officer. The inmates at HMP Bullingdon look forward to Friday, when they receive small treats like chocolate and shower gel - but dealing in these items quickly leads to violence(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

Meanwhile, James Layte's attention is diverted towards a historic Almanac(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: The Gandhi family answer top 10 questions on subjects including EastEnders, The Jungle Book and Formula One, as they try to score the 'Perfect 10'. Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Karen, Ben, Ali and David work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla and Andrea lock horns, Tracy hears the worst news, and Graham attempts to make amends(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dev is stunned to find Asha has been trying to lighten her skin, and Michelle tells Robert how Ray made a pass at her and is now making her life hell. Meanwhile, Kevin tells Debbie that he can't accept the money their Aunt Vi left(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: After finding an unusual tattoo on the college dean's body and two more murder victims with the same inking, Lewis and Hathaway discover that the tattoo signifies membership of a secret esoteric society that believes past sins can be forgiven by the literal sharing of guilt. Before the fourth and final murder takes place, the team must uncover the guilty secret that the victims share in order to learn the identity of the killer. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott head the cast of this action drama, now well into its third and final season. Murtaugh (Wayans) takes a family holiday, but is pulled back into work after witnessing an alleged abduction. Meanwhile, Cole (Scott) is left to investigate a murder by himself. Later, however, the two cases begin to intersect. Then, Erica's mother, Senator Malick comes to town and stirs things up between her daughter, Cole and the case at hand. Kevin Rahm, Johnathan Fernandez and Michelle Mitchenor also star(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Builder Rich from Birmingham, blacksmith Andy from Glastonbury, business manager Ashley from London and estate agent Ross from Huddersfield all vie for the attentions of 30 single women, intent on capturing their interest and securing a date. However, first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness monitors their progress and reviews the dating action from last week's couples on the Isle of Fernando's(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: South Africa v Italy. Jill Douglas presents action from the Pool B encounter at Shizuoka Stadium Ecopa in Japan. With analysis from David Flatman and Shane Williams, and commentary by Miles Harrison(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: As police comb the Le Saux haulage yard for clues, Nancy realises to her horror that she's left incriminating evidence at the scene. Worse follows when she learns her mother has gone missing from her psychiatric ward. In due course, as the shocking contents of the truck are revealed, the case seems close to being solved. However, Nancy knows there's more - and that the killer is still at large. Karla Crome, Robert James-Collier and Lindsey Coulson star(n)

Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Richard Osman, Gregg Wallace and Penny Lancaster to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a countdown of 100 talent-themed clips sent in by viewers, featuring everything from karaoke calamities to stage falls and battle stunts gone wrong, as magicians, acrobats, jugglers, dancers, singers and musicians vie for the number-one spot(n)

programme.title: Beethoven (1992)
programme.desc: An all-American family adopts a huge St Bernard, but the father is dismayed when it turns into a one-dog disaster zone. He insists the mutt must go to a new home, but when it is kidnapped by a crooked vet, dad has a change of heart and races to the rescue. Comedy, starring Charles Grodin, Bonnie Hunt, Oliver Platt and David Duchovny(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A daytime talk show uncovers a secret from Francine's past, revealing that she once fell down a well as an infant and was rescued by a fireman who gave his life to save hers. Animated comedy, with the guest voices of Will Forte and Michelle Dockery(n)

Dance drama sequel, starring Rick Malambri, Adam G Sevani and Sharni Vinson(n)

programme.title: Jurassic Park (1993)
programme.desc: Scientists are given a sneak preview of a safari park inhabited by genetically engineered dinosaurs created by an eccentric tycoon. However, it all goes wrong when a greedy employee sabotages the security systems, allowing the prehistoric beasts to roam free and hunt the visitors. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, starring Sam Neill, Laura Dern, Jeff Goldblum, Richard Attenborough and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter runs up a massive bill at the pharmacy and decides to sell Meg to the Goldmans to help pay it off - much to their son Neil's delight. However, she becomes jealous when he starts dating someone else. Meanwhile, Stewie falls in love with his new babysitter(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois is sent to prison for persistent shoplifting, plunging the family into such disarray that Peter feels obliged to help her escape. Forced to go on the run, they take refuge in 'Asian Town' - where Joe eventually tracks them down(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A trio of stories inspired by viewers' letters, including a tale in which Peter appears with his pals as vintage screen comedy team the Little Rascals, and another in which the Griffins gain superpowers - much to the neighbourhood's astonishment(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Ambrose bamboozles Rigsby by claiming to be a mystic, and soon has the other lodgers under a strange spell. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Dervish and Co are back at the crease but with ball tampering and rule bending rife, a fair game seems out of the question. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger worries about who will make the team's teas when Mim is taken ill. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah argue about what they should tell Annie about the farmhouse. Eric's attempts at reconciliation with Elizabeth are not working(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Archie is upset at the way Zoe has treated him, Frank is furious with Joe, Chris warns Joe to stay away from Kim, and Annie feels bitter towards her family(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken offers Deirdre financial help but she refuses it, Jack treats Vera to a day in Blackpool, and Rita is annoyed by the way Jenny takes advantage of Mavis(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey believes Alf cannot live without the shop, while Liz and Jim call a family conference, and then spend the evening together(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The villagers of Aspern Tallow begin to believe the ghost of a former resident has come back to haunt them. Barnaby and Troy have to determine why packing cases are apparently combusting spontaneously - and whether it is more than a coincidence that the battered body of a relative of the dead man has been found in the family vault. With John Nettles, Prunella Scales and Patricia Brake(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for harvest time, but Steph struggles to cope with the workload and faces losing the farm if she cannot meet her quota. A newcomer to the community stirs up tension between Sarah and Frances, while Joyce and the Brindsleys also receive unexpected visitors. Teresa faces a big decision, while Sarah finds herself in a dangerous situation. Alison discovers the real reason behind the parachute accidents and makes a horrifying connection to the factory. She races to warn Frances before it is too late(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The village prepares for Nick and Teresa's wedding as the Battle of Britain draws to an end. Pat faces a big decision as Marek prepares to leave, but Bob intends to ruin her plans. Frances fights to clear her name following the closure of the factory, Alison wrestles with her conscience, and a chance encounter leaves Joyce with some unanswered questions(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Following the loss of Leopard's Den, only Danny and Du Plessis remain in South Africa, desperate to raise enough money to buy a new reserve. When pregnant Alice arrives from the UK unannounced, she realises the pair have been lying to her about their jobs, and things go from bad to worse for Danny when a confrontation with Peeters pushes him to breaking point. Robert Bathurst (Cold Feet) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis is haunted by his belief that Danny murdered their old enemy Christian Peeters, and the friendship between the two men is tested to its limit. Meanwhile, Caroline, Liv and Charlotte return, only to find their new home falling apart and a mysterious illness affecting the vultures and animals on the reserve. African-set wildlife drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Deon Stewardson and Hayley Mills(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A nostalgic Stan remembers his first day at the depot, when he was a conductor and Blakey was a driver. He also recounts the events leading to Arthur and Olive's engagement. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Ormerod returns to work after his accident, but when his brusque treatment of a hypochondriac schoolgirl ends in tragedy, it's clear he still needs time to recover. New nurse Catherine Deane starts her rounds on the wards, and Mr Middleditch has a surprise announcement to make. Robert Daws, Amelia Curtis and Ian Carmichael star(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Mr Middleditch brings his reign to an end and hands over the ledgers to the new administrator - having craftily secured the hospital's future. Dr Ormerod's plaster comes off, Mr Rose risks his own skin to save a friend during a game of golf, and a trivial accident leads a family to make a major and heartbreaking discovery. Ian Carmichael, Robert Cavanah, Robert Daws and Wendy Craig star(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Toby Jones and Phyllis Logan(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver(n)

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The five-strong staff of a struggling Leeds supermarket face an uncertain future, so two of them come up with a plan to take matters into their own hands. But then the unthinkable happens - the group's numbers come up on the lottery and £18million falls into their laps overnight. It could be the answer to all their problems. Or life may be about to get a lot more complicated. Kay Mellor drama, starring Timothy Spall, Matthew McNulty, Joanna Page, Lorraine Bruce and Matthew Lewis(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey cuts the canteen's opening hours, but when he sacks one of the staff, things really turn nasty. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his father's glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerod's strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The villagers of Aspern Tallow begin to believe the ghost of a former resident has come back to haunt them. Barnaby and Troy have to determine why packing cases are apparently combusting spontaneously - and whether it is more than a coincidence that the battered body of a relative of the dead man has been found in the family vault. With John Nettles, Prunella Scales and Patricia Brake(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: When Sharon and Tracey discover Dorien has been keeping a secret for more than 50 years, they decide to get involved, but the novelist is reluctant to confront her past. Meanwhile, Tracey finds motherhood isn't all it's cracked up to be. Sitcom, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is nominated by Auntie Vera to take over her Pearly Queen title, but when Sharon finds out, she tries to outperform her sister to bag the role for herself. Meanwhile, Dorien reluctantly agrees to a family reunion. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph(n)

Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Toby Jones and Phyllis Logan(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver(n)

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The five-strong staff of a struggling Leeds supermarket face an uncertain future, so two of them come up with a plan to take matters into their own hands. But then the unthinkable happens - the group's numbers come up on the lottery and £18million falls into their laps overnight. It could be the answer to all their problems. Or life may be about to get a lot more complicated. Kay Mellor drama, starring Timothy Spall, Matthew McNulty, Joanna Page, Lorraine Bruce and Matthew Lewis(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey cuts the canteen's opening hours, but when he sacks one of the staff, things really turn nasty. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his father's glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerod's strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The villagers of Aspern Tallow begin to believe the ghost of a former resident has come back to haunt them. Barnaby and Troy have to determine why packing cases are apparently combusting spontaneously - and whether it is more than a coincidence that the battered body of a relative of the dead man has been found in the family vault. With John Nettles, Prunella Scales and Patricia Brake(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.title: World Cup Rivalries
programme.desc: A look at the football rivalry between the nations(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is persuaded to shadow a defecting civil servant who has been duped into carrying confidential papers to East Germany. Guest starring Ronald Hines(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A convict uses a medical experiment as an opportunity to escape, and comes to New York seeking revenge - but does not realise that he has been infected with a deadly virus(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A coroner asks Quincy to help him find the evidence he needs to close down a substandard foster home responsible for the death of a 7 year-old boy(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: South Africa v Italy (Kick-off 10.45am). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the Pool B fixture at Shizuoka Stadium Ecopa. programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry arrives at a harbour brewery to confronts drug dealers who know the whereabouts of a huge stash of heroin. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A private detective sets himself up as a potential target for a hired killer, and does not take kindly to Theo's attempts to rescue him. Guest starring Bill Duke (Black Lightning)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner investigates the murder of an elderly woman, but is himself assaulted and savagely beaten while searching for evidence to convict the victim's killers(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry are hired by two farmers to transport a prize bull, little suspecting they are actually being tricked into stealing it. When their error of judgement become apparent, the duo try to redeem themselves by working out a way to get the animal back. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle suspect Russian intervention when a mentally unstable lieutenant colonel holding valuable Nato secrets disappears from an army psychiatric hospital. Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter stake out a dodgy garage, believed to be the base of a criminal gang. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star in the first episode of the crime-busting drama, with Ian Hendry, Brian Blessed and June Brown(n)

programme.desc: South Africa v Italy. Jill Douglas presents action from the Pool B encounter at Shizuoka Stadium Ecopa in Japan. With analysis from David Flatman and Shane Williams, and commentary by Miles Harrison(n)

programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley are tasked with championing two of the best four-seater performance cars released in 1976 - the Volkswagen Golf GTi and the Saab 99 Turbo(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney arrive in Texas, where they enjoy driving around in tanks, experience space travel in a centrifuge and hear campfire tales about UFOs. First up, the pair undertake some centrifuge training, which is used to prepare astronauts for the crushing gravitational forces produced at take-off from the extreme exhilaration. They also head to the outskirts of Houston to meet some UFO enthusiasts who investigate claimed sightings of extra-terrestrials, and are gripped by stories of spaceships blocking roads and people being taken by aliens for experiments(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney embark on the final leg of their journey, which sees the pair arrive in the Louisiana city of New Orleans. Here, they visit a spiritual voodoo priest for a spot of energy cleansing, and feed alligators in the Bayou with Captain Billy, who knows every corner of the wetlands, having been born and raised on the bayous of South Louisiana. Later, Bradley hops on board the Natchez jazz Steamboat, before the pair take to the streets to lead a spectacular parade with a six-piece band(n)

programme.title: Crimson Tide (1995)
programme.desc: A veteran US submarine captain receives an incomplete message after a Russian base falls into rebel hands, and is convinced it is an order to fire his nuclear weapons. However, his first officer refuses to take such potentially devastating action without confirmed orders, leading to a deadly battle of wills. Thriller, starring Gene Hackman, Denzel Washington and Viggo Mortensen(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley get behind the wheel of two sports cars that saved their brands from going bankrupt in 1996 - the Porsche Boxster and the Lotus Elise(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of the 3.10 Challenge Cup from Ascot, and races at 2.00, 2.35 and 3.40. Plus, races from Newmarket at 1.40, 2.15 and 3.25, and 2.55 from Redcar. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reporting by Sally Ann Grassick, Mick FitzGerald and Rishi Persad, and commentary from Mark Johnson.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at how the how the hovercraft inspired the invention of one of Britain's best-selling lawnmowers, an insight into the making of a suitcase beloved by royalty, Arctic explorers and Hollywood celebrities. Plus, what it takes to craft a spoon fit for the Downing Street dinner table, and the bangers that turned the sausage status-quo on its head. Ricky Tomlinson narrates(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Vintage restorers Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove challenge themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's unwanted automotive junk and classic collectibles. They begin at Bristol Classic Car Show, where they acquire a quirky car from the 1970s, but making a quick profit does not prove easy(n)

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.desc: The team prepares for a huge auto auction where they hope they can sell some stock for big profits. Perry and Eric work on two vehicles they think will sell for big bucks - a 1931 Hot Rod and a 1953 Merc. However, first transmission problems ensue with the Hot Rod, followed by a paint job that goes horribly wrong on the Mercedes(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the latest matches in Japan - England v Argentina at Tokyo Stadium, Australia v Uruguay at Oita Stadium and Japan v Samoa at City of Toyota Stadium(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless hero plans the Christmas holiday of a lifetime - but fate intervenes and pulls the rug out from under his feet, reducing what should have been the perfect festive season to a frantic comedy of errors. Slapstick, starring Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's comic alter ego embarks on another disaster-filled outing when he treats his hotel room to a makeover but ends up causing devastation(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Monty is concerned about Sammy's career relaunch, and Kenneth's romantic troubles continue as he tries to put off Vladimir's advances. Meanwhile, the Dawsons are on edge about the arrival of Cyd's father Franco, and Noreen and Pauline return to the Solana, and Pauline's writers block soon disappears when she finds her muse(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 4 (1998)
programme.desc: Mismatched LAPD detectives Riggs and Murtaugh stumble across a ship transporting Chinese immigrants into the US. As they try to get to the bottom of the organisation behind the smuggling, they find themselves up against a master criminal who is an expert in martial arts and heads a triad responsible for people-trafficking and forgery. Action adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Rene Russo, Joe Pesci, Jet Li and Chris Rock(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Conspirators (1978)
programme.desc: A terrorist posing as an Irish author heads a peace organization while secretly running guns for the IRA in Belfast. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Clive Revill, Jeanette Nolan and Bernard Behrens(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the 'lau-lau', others the 'piraiba'. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermen's tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster(n)

programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney arrive in Texas, where they enjoy driving around in tanks, experience space travel in a centrifuge and hear campfire tales about UFOs. First up, the pair undertake some centrifuge training, which is used to prepare astronauts for the crushing gravitational forces produced at take-off from the extreme exhilaration. They also head to the outskirts of Houston to meet some UFO enthusiasts who investigate claimed sightings of extra-terrestrials, and are gripped by stories of spaceships blocking roads and people being taken by aliens for experiments(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney embark on the final leg of their journey, which sees the pair arrive in the Louisiana city of New Orleans. Here, they visit a spiritual voodoo priest for a spot of energy cleansing, and feed alligators in the Bayou with Captain Billy, who knows every corner of the wetlands, having been born and raised on the bayous of South Louisiana. Later, Bradley hops on board the Natchez jazz Steamboat, before the pair take to the streets to lead a spectacular parade with a six-piece band(n)

programme.title: Crimson Tide (1995)
programme.desc: A veteran US submarine captain receives an incomplete message after a Russian base falls into rebel hands, and is convinced it is an order to fire his nuclear weapons. However, his first officer refuses to take such potentially devastating action without confirmed orders, leading to a deadly battle of wills. Thriller, starring Gene Hackman, Denzel Washington and Viggo Mortensen(n)

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Georgie Barrat jets off to Germany, where she gets her hands on all the latest gadgets at the IFA, Europe's biggest tech show. Jon Bentley tears up the tarmac with the help of stunt driver Paul Swift, to road test the latest dash-cams. The G Team sets about making a bed and breakfast in Torquay, Devon, more tech savvy, while Ortis Deley delves into the archives to recall what life was like before games consoles. Retired traditionalists the Three Millers plan to re-enact a dramatic railway scene from 1950s Holland, while the Titfield Thunderbolts attempt to stage an action adventure in the jungle involving a plane crash and a volcano. Presented by James Richardson and Tim Shaw(n)

programme.title: Inside the Cockpit: The Concorde Crash
programme.desc: The incident from July 2000 when an Air France Concorde crashed shortly after take-off from Charles de Gaulle Airport. All 109 passengers and crew members on board were killed, as well as at least four people on the ground. The entire world reeled in shock as in 24 years of commercial flight, Concorde had an unblemished record and was considered to be one of the safest passenger planes in history. This documentary re-examines the causes of the incident, as revealed by French and British air crash investigators(n)

programme.title: Celebs Go Motorhoming: Back on the Road

programme.title: Most Shocking TV Moments

programme.title: The Nile: Egypt's Great River with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: Bettany visits the west bank of the Nile opposite Luxor where, for 500 years, the Ancient Egyptians buried their pharaohs in the Valley of the Kings, among them the boy-king Tutankhamun. However, the historian crosses the local hills to the Workers' Village, where generations of skilled royal tomb-builders lived. As it turns out, they also dumped masses of domestic rubbish, which is now giving some insight into the highs, lows and preoccupations of ancient Egyptians. Bettany then heads south on the Nile's oldest steam ship SS Sudan, which inspired Agatha Christie to write Death on the Nile(n)

Featuring contributions from Louis Walsh, Shaun Ryder, Bez, Michael Burke, Darren Day, Gail Porter, Tim Vincent, Liz McClarnon and Kerry Katona(n)

programme.title: The Fighting Seabees (1944)

However, when one recklessly causes a tragedy, he seeks redemption by launching an attack on a tank battalion. Drama, starring John Wayne, Susan Hayward and Dennis O'Keefe(n)

programme.title: The Comancheros (1961)

However, the former serviceman's darker impulses remain hard to control, especially when confronted with evidence of the cult leader's dishonesty. Paul Thomas Anderson's drama, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Philip Seymour Hoffman and Amy Adams(n)

In spite of disagreements and many ups and downs, the teacher gradually forms a deep platonic love for her royal employer. Period drama, starring Jodie Foster, Chow Yun-Fat, Ling Bai, Syed Alwi, Tom Felton and Randall Duk Kim(n)

programme.title: The Dam Busters (1955)

Second World War drama, with Michael Redgrave, Richard Todd, Basil Sydney, Derek Farr and Ursula Jeans(n)

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992)

Having got his hands on his father's credit card, he is enjoying the good life staying in a luxury hotel - until he bumps into two familiar burglars with an axe to grind. Comedy sequel, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, Brenda Fricker and Tim Curry(n)

programme.title: The Maze Runner (2014)

When another new arrival brings an ominous message, he realises time is running out for the group and they must find an escape route through a deadly labyrinth if they are to survive. Sci-fi adventure based on James Dashner's novel, starring Dylan O'Brien, Kaya Scodelario and Patricia Clarkson(n)
